conversation_summary.user_role: Product manager with Shopify development experience, helping a friend build a portfolio
conversation_summary.friend_profile: Film and video editor who wants a portfolio website
key_requirements: YouTube video integration - auto-fetch videos from channel
Embedded video player on the site (not just links to YouTube)
Automatic sync when new videos uploaded to YouTube
About me page with bio and experience
Work portfolio page showing video grid
technical_decisions: Chose custom static site over Wix/Webflow for free hosting
Using Astro framework for static site generation
YouTube Data API v3 for fetching videos
Netlify for hosting and form handling
No CMS initially (can add Decap CMS later if needed)

completed: Tailwind CSS integrated (npx astro add tailwind)
Project structure created with src/pages, src/styles folders
All code files designed and ready to be created
pending: Create actual files in the project directory
User needs to get YouTube API credentials
User needs to get YouTube Channel ID
Customize content (names, URLs, bio, contact info)
Test locally with npm run dev

environment.notes: User needs to fill this after getting credentials from Google Cloud Console

layouts.purpose: Main layout wrapper for all pages
key_features: Global styles for black background theme

components.purpose: Fixed navigation header with links and social icons
key_features: Active state highlighting for current page

components.purpose: Display YouTube videos in responsive grid with modal player
key_features: Modal popup for embedded video playback
technical_details: Uses YouTube embed URLs for playback

pages.purpose: Homepage with hero section and video grid
key_features: Hero section with name and tagline
Fetches videos at build time using getChannelVideos()
Displays VideoGrid component with fetched videos
Shows error message if no videos found

pages.purpose: About page with bio, experience, and skills
key_features: Skills tags (Premiere Pro, After Effects, etc.)

pages.purpose: Contact page with Netlify form and contact info
key_features: Form fields: name, email, project type, message
customization_needed: Update social media URLs (YouTube, Instagram, LinkedIn)
technical_notes: Form automatically works when deployed to Netlify
Submissions appear in Netlify dashboard under Forms

key_features: Returns formatted video objects with thumbnails, titles, embed URLs

steps.details: Create new project or select existing
Search for 'YouTube Data API v3' in APIs & Services and enable it

methods: Method 1: Go to channel, look at URL: youtube.com/channel/{CHANNEL_ID}

deployment_instructions.why_netlify: Free tier, automatic HTTPS, form handling, easy deployment

details: Click 'Add new site' → 'Import an existing project'
Build settings: Build command = 'npm run build', Publish directory = 'dist'
Add environment variables: PUBLIC_YOUTUBE_API_KEY and PUBLIC_YOUTUBE_CHANNEL_ID

checks: Check Netlify dashboard → Forms for submission

optional_auto_rebuild.purpose: Auto-update site when new YouTube videos uploaded
steps: Netlify: Build & deploy → Build hooks → Create build hook
Use cron-job.org to schedule daily rebuilds
Or use Netlify scheduled functions (paid plans)

components.buttons: px-8 py-4 bg-white text-black rounded-lg hover:bg-gray-200

astro.why: Fast static site generation, zero JS by default, SEO friendly

youtube_api.cost_per_request: ~1 unit for channel, ~1 unit for playlist
youtube_api.build_time_fetch: Videos fetched during build, not on client side

important_notes.api_quotas: YouTube API has daily quota limits. Building once fetches all videos and caches them statically.
important_notes.security: .env file should be in .gitignore (already is by default). Add API keys to Netlify separately.
customization_priority: 3. Update email and phone in contact page
important_notes.testing: Always run 'npm run dev' locally before deploying
important_notes.builds: Run 'npm run build' to test production build locally

commands_reference.development: npm run dev (starts local server at localhost:4321)
commands_reference.build: npm run build (creates production build in dist/)
commands_reference.preview: npm run preview (preview production build locally)

videos_not_loading: Check .env file exists and has correct values
Verify YouTube Data API v3 is enabled in Google Cloud
Check API key has no restrictions (or allows your domain)
Verify Channel ID is correct (starts with UC...)
Check browser console for error messages
build_errors: Delete node_modules and package-lock.json, run npm install
Check for syntax errors in .astro files
netlify_deploy_fails: Check build settings: command='npm run build', directory='dist'
Verify environment variables are set in Netlify dashboard
Check deploy logs in Netlify for specific errors

5. Create src/lib/youtube.js with the provided code
6. Create/replace src/pages/index.astro with the provided code
7. Create src/pages/about.astro with the provided code
8. Create src/pages/contact.astro with the provided code
9. Create .env.example as template (don't create actual .env - user will do it)
10. Verify all files are created correctly
11. Wait for user to add YouTube API credentials to .env
12. Help user test with npm run dev
full_file_contents_reference: All complete file contents are available in the files shared in the previous chat. Each file is fully written and ready to be created as-is, only requiring customization of placeholders like 'Deepti Valunjkar', email addresses, and social media URLs.
